summary: Victorian novelist. Known for North and South, Cranford, Mary Barton, Ruth, and her biography of Charlotte Bronte. Serialized works in Dickens's Household Words.

works.name: North and South
works.description: Margaret Hale moves from Helstone to industrial Milton; falls in love with mill-owner John Thornton.

clues.clue: Margaret Hale moves from Helstone to Milton (Darkshire); marries mill-owner John Thornton
examples: Margaret Hale moves from her hometown Helstone to the Darkshire town of Milton
falls in love with Mr Thornton

clues.clue: Thornton shuts down mill due to a strike; Nicholas Higgins is union leader
examples: John Thornton, the owner of a cotton mill
meets the Union leader Nicholas Higgins

clues.clue: Margaret steps out to quell rioters during a strike
examples: the main female character steps out of a barricaded house to quell the rioters
considered calling it Death and Variations

works.name: Cranford
works.description: Episodic novel about a small town of genteel ladies, narrated by Mary Smith.

clues.clue: Miss Matty goes bankrupt; sells tea after bank collapse; narrated by Mary Smith
examples: Miss Matty, who must sell tea after losing her money in a bank collapse

clues.clue: Story of a cat swallowing lace; Betsy Barker dresses a cow in flannel
examples: a cat who swallowed a piece of lace
Betsy Barker attempts to save a cow that had fallen into a lime-pit by dressing it in flannel

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Mary Barton, Ruth, biography of Charlotte Bronte, ghost stories.

clues.clue: Biography of Charlotte Bronte — omitted details; blamed Lydia Robinson for Branwell's death
examples: blames Lydia Robinson for the death of its subject's brother Branwell

clues.clue: Mary Barton — Jem Wilson; Job Legh collects insects; industrial Manchester
examples: the reconciliation between a mill-owning and a mill-working family

clues.clue: Ruth — orphan Ruth seduced by Henry Bellingham; becomes a nurse
examples: the title character becomes a nurse to typhoid patients

clues.clue: Serialized in Dickens's Household Words; Dickens called her 'dear Scheherazade'
examples: Dickens described this writer as 'dear Scheherazade'

clues.clue: Ghost stories including 'The Old Nurse's Story'
examples: first made her name with ghost stories like 'The Old Nurse's Story'

comprehensive_summary: Elizabeth Gaskell wrote North and South (Margaret Hale moves to industrial Milton and marries Thornton), Cranford (Miss Matty goes bankrupt), Mary Barton, Ruth, and a biography of Charlotte Bronte. She serialized works in Dickens's Household Words.

cards.front: Novel: Margaret Hale moves from rural Helstone to industrial Milton
Novel: episodic novel about genteel ladies
Novel: Miss Matty goes bankrupt and sells tea
Author: wrote biography of Charlotte Bronte
Author: serialized in Dickens's Household Words
